PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE FULL AND COMPLETE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO THE REQUEST FOR DOCUMENTS, REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS AND FORM INTERROGATORIES SERVED BY DEFENDANT GIN, WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO PAY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,600 TO DEFENDANT GIN, WITH THE SANCTIONS TO BE PAID WITHIN 20 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. DISCOVERY 302, MOTION IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF SANJEEV RAI DBA IMPRINT CP IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. TERMINATING SANCTIONS ARE APPROPRIATE FOR PLAINTIFF'S REPEATED FAILURE TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY AND THE COURT ORDER OF NOVEMBER 20, 2015 COMPELLING DISCOVERY AND THE PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS. THE REQUEST FOR ADDITIONAL SANCTIONS OF $3,960 FOR THE FILING OF THIS MOTION IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE THE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: PHILIP ANDERSEN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Moton In Support Of Defendant And Cross-Complainants Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling for DEFENDANT MARK GIN DBA COPY EDGE's Motion In Support Of Defendant And Cross-Complainants Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Sanctions. The motion is granted. The complaint of Plaintiff Sanjeev Rai DBA Imprint CP is dismissed with prejudice. Plaintiff did not oppose the motion. Terminating sanctions are appropriate for Plaintiff's repeated failure to respond to discovery and the court order of November 20, 2015 compelling discovery and the payment of sanctions. The request for additional sanctions of $3,960 for the filing of this motion is denied. Prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Philip Andersen; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Request For Documents, Request For Admissions, And Form Interrogatories, To Compel Plaintiff Sanjeev Rai To Appear And Testify For Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL M. CHING, ESQ. of Ching & Yamamoto LLP, Tel No. 510-922-9471 appearing for Defendant Mark Gin dba Copy Edge;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Defense counsel stipulates to matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Mark Gin's Motion to Compel Plaintiff Sanjeev Rai DBA Imprint CP to Provide Further Discovery Responses to Defendant's Request for Documents, Request for Admissions, and Form Interrogatories, to Compel Plaintiff to Appear and Testify For Deposition and For Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff is ordered to provide full and complete verified responses to the Request for Documents, Request for Admissions and Form Interrogatories served by Defendant Gin, within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition on a mutually agreeable date within 45 days of notice of entry of this order. Plaintiff and Plaintiff's counsel are ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $5,600 to Defendant Gin, with the sanctions to be paid within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Margaret Niver, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
